rulings.Case Title: FRANCES SUSSNA VS. PRIME OLD COUNTY LP et al
rulings.Rulings: HOUSING COURT LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 23, 2014. Line 2. DEFENDANT KARI RILEY DEMURRER's TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Second amended complaint relates back to the filing of the original complaint. Plaintiff alleges that she learned the facts giving rise to Riley's liability in April 2013. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges in the alternative (Fenton/Riley) causes of action for Trespass, Conversion, Elder Abuse, UCL. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GARY PASQUINELLI et al VS. NORA A. YEGEHYAIAN et al
rulings.Rulings: HOUSING COURT LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 23, 2014. Line 3. DEFENDANT NORA YEGHYAIAN's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Continued to May 16, 2014. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B and C and provide courtesy copy of the operative complaint and reply no later than April 30, 2014 with cover letter reflecting new hearing date. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: THE FRONT STREET BUILDING LLC VS. TELMATE LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: HOUSING COURT LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 23, 2014. Line 4. PLAINTIFF THE FRONT STREET BUILDING LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication DENIED. Moving party fails to shift burden and show that "The Lease bars constructive eviction as a remedy for tenant" under all circumstances or under the circumstances of this case. "Ordinarily, the statute invalidates contracts that purport to exempt an individual or entity from liability for future intentional wrongs and gross negligence. "Whether an exculpatory clause 'covers a given case turns primarily on contractual interpretation, and it is the intent of the parties as expressed in the agreement that should control. When the parties knowingly bargain for the protection at issue, the protection should be afforded. This requires an inquiry into the circumstances of the damage or injury and the language of the contract; of necessity, each case will turn on its own facts.' " Frittelli, Inc v. 350 North Canon Drive, LP (2011) 202 Cal.App.4th 35, 43. No evidence is provided to show that Telmate knowingly contracted away its right to defend against intentional tortious acts. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN BALDINI VS. MANUEL RAMIREZ et al
rulings.Rulings: HOUSING COURT LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 23, 2014. Line 7. DEFENDANT MANUEL RAMIREZ's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled, 5 days to answer. D fails to cite any authority for the proposition that inclusion of the request to enter invalidates the "3 Day Notice" for the purposes of UD action. =(501/REQ)
